name: bean
description: A documentation-only Codex plugin adding one explicit /bean skill: run a task as a recursive convergence loop — investigate, record evidence in a ledger, compile, revise beliefs, and loop until converged.

interface.shortDescription: Explicit /bean recursive convergence loop for careful tasks
interface.longDescription: bean is a provider-neutral skill that runs a task as a recursive convergence loop: investigate the most decisive open question, record what you learn as typed evidence in a ledger, compile to score what is still weak or contradictory, revise beliefs the evidence overturns, and loop until it converges. It ships bean-check, a zero-dependency Node CLI that scores convergence and exits nonzero until it is reached; where it cannot run, bean falls back to a hand-checked bean-stalk.md ledger. No servers, hooks, network calls, runtime dependencies, or telemetry (TypeScript is a CI-only devDependency). Modeled on how the Fable model worked; a loop discipline, not a capability transplant.
defaultPrompt: Use /bean to investigate, record evidence, and loop until the answer converges.
Use /bean to revise beliefs as new evidence overturns earlier claims.
Use /bean to open a cross-model blindspot review on high-stakes work.
